content.full_text:
State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 207 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance • • Administration • • Executive/Legal • • Enforcement
(916) 322-5662      322-5660        322-5901        322-6441

August 9, 1984


Ronald J. Einboden
City Attorney
City of Covina
c/o Oliver, Stoever & Laskin
1000 Sunset Boulevard
Los Angeles, CA  90012

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our No. A-84-192

Dear Mr. Einboden:

You have requested advice on behalf of Covina City
Councilmember Jerry Edgar, who also serves as a Board member of
the Community Redevelopment Agency.

FACTS

The facts are as follows:

1. Mr. Edgar is a member of the Covina City Council
and the Covina Community Redevelopment Agency (CRA).

2. The Covina Redevelopment Area Project Area No. 1
was adopted on or about 1974.

3. Within that area, the CRA and the City Council are
currently considering a concept of an "Auto Row."

4. The Auto Row concept, if brought into fruition,
would require the acquisition and/or condemnation of
approximately 7 approved parcels of real property.

5. Mr. Edgar is the beneficiary of a Trust Deed on one
of the subject properties. The value of the Trust Deed
exceeds $1,000.

Ronald J. Einboden
August 9, 1984
Page 2


ANALYSIS

Mr. Edgar has an "interest in real property" as defined in
the Political Reform Act/ (the "Act"). Section 82033. His
interest is a beneficial one. You have stated that the value of
the Trust Deed exceeds $1,000; consequently, the threshold level
of economic interest has been satisfied.

An official has a financial interest in a decision
within the meaning of Section 87100 if it is reasonably
foreseeable that the decision will have a material
financial effect, distinguishable from its effect on
the public generally, on:

* * *

(b) Any real property in which the public
official has a direct or indirect interest worth more
than one thousand dollars ($1,000).

Thus, Mr. Edgar must disqualify himself as to decisions of
either the City Council or the Community Redevelopment Agency if
the decisions will have a reasonably foreseeable material
financial effect upon the real property for which he holds the
Trust Deed. (Section 87100.) We assume that these seven
parcels will be affected in a manner which is distinguishable
from the effect of the decisions on the public generally.
(Section 87103.) Consequently, the only question remaining to
be resolved is that of material financial effect.

Commission regulation 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18702 (copy
enclosed) provides guidance on this question; particularly
subdivision (b)(2).

If the effect of any given decision (or series of
interrelated decisions) on the real property in question will be
to raise or lower its fair market value by at least $1,000 then
it may be material. If the effect will increase or decrease the
fair market value by at least $10,000, then the effect will be
deemed material. If the reasonably foreseeable effect will be
between $1,000 and $10,000, then it will be deemed material if

_________________
1/ Government Code Sections 81000-91014. All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise
specified.

Ronald J. Einboden
August 9, 1984
Page 3


the amount of change is at least one-half of one percent of the
current fair market value. To illustrate: if the anticipated
change in value as a result of the decision is $5,000, the
effect is material if the fair market value of the property is
$1,000,000 or less. If the current fair market value is greater
than $1,000,000, then a $5,000 effect would not be considered
material.

     Presumably, the obligor under the Deed of Trust is a source
of income to Mr. Edgar in excess of $250 paid or promised during
the last 12 months. (Section 87103(c).) Therefore, it is also
important to consider the reasonably foreseeable effects of any
decision on that person or entity. The test to be applied is
that found in subdivision (b)(3) of regulation 2 Cal. Adm. Code
Section 18702. Again, if materiality is found, the
disqualification will be required.

     If you have questions regarding the advice in this letter,
please call me at (916) 322-5901.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        [signature]
                                        Robert E. Lardigh
                                        Counsel
                                        Legal Division

REL:plh
Enclosure

LAW OFFICES

OLIVER, STOEVER & LASKIN
A PROFESSIONAL CORPORATION

1000 SUNSET BOULEVARD

LOS ANGELES, CALIFORNIA 90012

(213) 250-3043

THOMAS W. STOEVER

RICHARD LASKIN

WILLIAM S. BARR

RONALD J. EIMBODEN

CHARLES S. VOSE

CONNIE COOKE SANDIFER

ROBERTA S. KALLAN

OF COUNSEL:

NORMAN G. OLIVER

JUL 24 1954

July 18, 1984


Barbara Milman
General Counsel
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804

Re: Request for Advice Letter

Dear Ms. Milman:

     I am the City Attorney for the City of Covina and
would request an Advice Letter on behalf of City Council
Member and Community Redevelopment Agency Board Member,
Jerry Edgar as to whether there would be a conflict of
interest pursuant to Government Code Section 87100 et
seq.

     The facts are as follows:

     1. Mr. Edgar is a member of the Covina City Council
        and the Covina Community Redevelopment Agency
        (CRA).

     2. The Covina Redevelopment Area Project Area No.
        1 was adopted on or about 1974.

     3. Within that area, the CRA and the City Council
        are currently considering a concept of an "Auto
        Row".

     4. The Auto Row concept, if brought into fruition,
        would require the acquisition and/or condemnation
        of approximately 7 approved parcels of real property.

     5. Mr. Edgar is the beneficiary of a Trust Deed on
        one of the subject properties. The value of the
        Trust Deed exceeds $1000.00.

OLIVER, STOEVER & LASKIN


Barbara Milman
Page Two
July 18, 1984


If you require further facts concerning this request
kindly contact the undersigned. It would be appreciated if
you would furnish your Advice Letter at the earliest possible
date.

Very truly yours,

Ronald D. Einboden
of OLIVER, STOEVER & LASKIN

RJE:caj

cc: Councilman Jerry Edgar

sections.question: Whether Covina City Councilmember and Community Redevelopment Agency Board Member Jerry Edgar, who is the beneficiary of a Trust Deed (value exceeding $1,000) on one of seven parcels of real property that would be affected by the proposed 'Auto Row' concept, would have a conflict of interest pursuant to Government Code Section 87100 et seq., and whether he must disqualify himself from decisions on this matter.
sections.conclusion: Mr. Edgar has an 'interest in real property' as defined in the Political Reform Act (Section 82033) with a beneficial interest exceeding $1,000 threshold. Mr. Edgar must disqualify himself as to decisions of either the City Council or the Community Redevelopment Agency if the decisions will have a reasonably foreseeable material financial effect upon the real property for which he holds the Trust Deed (Section 87100). The determination of material effect is guided by Commission regulation 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18702: if fair market value change is at least $10,000, the effect is deemed material; if between $1,000-$10,000, it is material if at least one-half of one percent of current fair market value; the obligor under the Deed of Trust must also be considered if it is a source of income exceeding $250 in the last 12 months.

sections.question_synthetic: Must a city councilmember and CRA board member disqualify himself from decisions regarding an 'Auto Row' redevelopment project if he holds a beneficial interest in a Trust Deed on one of the affected properties?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: Mr. Edgar must disqualify himself from decisions on the Auto Row project because he holds a beneficial interest in real property that will be materially affected by those decisions. Material effect is determined by comparing the anticipated change in fair market value ($10,000 threshold or one-half of one percent of value) and the effect on the obligor under the Deed of Trust.
sections.parsing_notes: Format: modern; LLM: This document contains both the incoming request letter (July 18, 1984) and the FPPC's formal response/advice letter (August 9, 1984). The question and conclusion sections are well-defined. The FPPC response clearly states the legal analysis and materiality test using Commission regulations.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether Covina City Councilmember and Community Redevelopment Agency Board Member Jerry Edgar, who is the beneficiary of a Trust Deed (value exceeding $1,000) on one of seven parcels of real property that would be affected by the proposed 'Auto Row' concept, would have a conflict of interest pursuant to Government Code Section 87100 et seq., and whether he must disqualify himself from decisions on this matter.

CONCLUSION: Mr. Edgar has an 'interest in real property' as defined in the Political Reform Act (Section 82033) with a beneficial interest exceeding $1,000 threshold. Mr. Edgar must disqualify himself as to decisions of either the City Council or the Community Redevelopment Agency if the decisions will have a reasonably foreseeable material financial effect upon the real property for which he holds the Trust Deed (Section 87100). The determination of material effect is guided by Commission regulation 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18702: if fair market value change is at least $10,000, the effect is deemed material; if between $1,000-$10,000, it is material if at least one-half of one percent of current fair market value; the obligor under the Deed of Trust must also be considered if it is a source of income exceeding $250 in the last 12 months.

Consequently, the only question remaining to be resolved is that of material financial effect. Commission regulation 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18702 (copy enclosed) provides guidance on this question; particularly subdivision (b)(2). If the effect will increase or decrease the fair market value by at least $10,000, then the effect will be deemed material. If the reasonably foreseeable effect will be between $1,000 and $10,000, then it will be deemed material if _________________
embedding.summary: The FPPC advises that Covina City Councilmember Jerry Edgar, who holds a beneficial interest in a Trust Deed on one of seven parcels potentially affected by a proposed 'Auto Row' redevelopment project, must disqualify himself from City Council and CRA decisions on the project due to a financial conflict of interest under Government Code Section 87100.
